commonMedications.name: Semaglutide / GLP-1 (Ozempic) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Covered under GMS medical card or Drugs Payment Scheme (€80/mo cap per family)

commonMedications.name: Atorvastatin 20mg [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: GMS or DPS; generic pricing applies

commonMedications.name: Atorvastatin 20mg [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: GMS or DPS; generic pricing applies

medicine.pharmacyAccess: Boots, LloydsPharmacy, and independent pharmacies throughout Ireland. Late-night pharmacies in cities. Prescription required for most medications.
medicine.notes: Ireland has the Drugs Payment Scheme (DPS) capping family medication costs at €80/month. Medical card holders (income-tested or over-70) pay nothing. Over-70s get automatic medical card. Prices shown assume DPS or medical card coverage.

plans.name: Google Fi Flexible (US Number)
plans.notes: Line 1 $20 + Line 2 $15 = $35 base. Data $10/GB × 3GB = $30. Works in Ireland at same rate. Keeps US number for banking/2FA.

plans.name: Three Ireland SIM-Only
plans.notes: 2 lines €12/line (~$13). Three has best 5G coverage. EU roaming included.

categories.name: Dining Out
categories.seniorDiscounts: Early bird menus widely available €15-25 for 2-3 courses.
categories.notes: Lunch for 2 at moderate restaurant ~€30-35, 2x/week = ~€260/mo = ~$290/mo in Wexford, Ireland.

categories.name: Cable + 1 Gig Internet
categories.seniorDiscounts: No specific senior discounts. Some providers offer loyalty rates.

categories.name: NFL RedZone
categories.notes: DAZN NFL Game Pass €19.99/mo during season. Sky Sports NFL alternative. Annualized ~$18/mo.

categories.name: Gym/Fitness
categories.seniorDiscounts: Some local leisure centres offer over-65 rates.

categories.name: Parks/Outdoors
categories.seniorDiscounts: OPW Heritage Card €40/yr (seniors €30) for all heritage sites.
categories.notes: Free parks, coastal walks, greenways, national parks.

categories.name: Books/Media
categories.seniorDiscounts: Public libraries free with library card.
categories.notes: Public libraries free. Budget for books, newspapers.

monthlyPass.notes: Irish residents 66+ qualify for Free Travel Pass (unlimited free public transport nationwide). Non-residents may not qualify immediately.

publicTransit.coverage: Bus Éireann intercity and local routes. Dublin Bus, Luas tram, and DART rail in Dublin. Limited rural transit.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: Irish residents 66+ qualify for Free Travel Pass (unlimited free public transport nationwide). Non-residents may not qualify immediately.

rideShare.availability: Uber available as taxi booking only (no UberX). FreeNow app for taxis. Traditional taxi ranks.

registration.notes: Annual motor tax based on CO2 emissions. NCT (National Car Test) required every 2 years.

insurance.notes: Third-party from ~$60/mo. Comprehensive $80-120/mo. Insurance costs are among the highest in Europe.

parking.notes: Pay-and-display in town centers. Resident permits available in some areas. Multi-story car parks $100-180/mo.

tolls.notes: Estimated monthly toll/highway costs for Wexford area.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: Irish residents 66+ qualify for Free Travel Pass (unlimited free public transport nationwide). Non-residents may not qualify immediately.

transportation.walkability: Walkable town centers, especially in smaller cities. Larger cities have good pedestrian infrastructure.

carFree.notes: Transit pass + occasional ride-share. Limited transit — may need more ride-share trips.

withCar.notes: Includes insurance, fuel, parking, maintenance. Moderate suburban costs.

eyeExam.notes: Eye test ~50 EUR. PRSI Treatment Benefit covers 1 free eye exam every 2 years for eligible contributors.

glasses.notes: Frames+lenses ~90 EUR with PRSI contribution. Progressive ~200 EUR. PRSI gives up to 51.50 EUR toward glasses every 2 years.

insurance.coverage: PRSI Treatment Benefit covers 1 exam/2yr + 51.50 EUR glasses contribution
insurance.notes: No separate vision insurance. Private health insurance (VHI, Laya, Irish Life) may include optical benefit. Medical card holders get free eye tests.

cleaning.notes: Scale and polish ~60 EUR. PRSI covers 1 free dental exam + cleaning per year for eligible PRSI contributors.

filling.notes: Composite filling ~100 EUR. PRSI does not cover fillings. Private dental plan recommended.

crown.notes: Porcelain crown ~600 EUR. Major work expensive; dental plans help with 25-40% discount.

insurance.coverage: DeCare Dental or equivalent, PRSI exam + cleaning, 30% off treatments
insurance.notes: Dental plans ~18 EUR/mo. DeCare is main Irish dental insurer. Medical card provides free dental for low-income residents.

items.name: Water
items.notes: Tap safe in most areas; mineral preferred

items.name: Premium dog food
items.notes: Royal Canin/Hills from pet shops or vet clinics

breakdown.notes: Renter's insurance not standard in most European countries. Utilities billed separately.

housing.taxNotes: No rental tax for tenants. LPT (Local Property Tax) is landlord responsibility. No stamp duty on residential leases.
housing.leaseTerms: Part 4 tenancy rights after 6 months (up to 6-year security). Rent Pressure Zones cap increases at 2%/year. Security deposit 1 month rent. 28-day notice minimum.
housing.marketNotes: Southeast coast town. Affordable by Irish standards. Good selection of 2-3BR houses.

stores.name: Tesco Ireland
stores.notes: Largest chain in Ireland. Superstore, Metro, and Express formats.

stores.name: SuperValu
stores.notes: Musgrave-owned. Strong community presence, good fresh food.

stores.name: Lidl
stores.notes: Budget-friendly. Popular bakery. Weekly themed promotions.

stores.name: Aldi
stores.notes: Growing presence. Good value on everyday items.

stores.name: Farmers markets
stores.notes: Weekly markets in most towns. Local artisan cheese, bread, organic produce.

healthcare.coverageSummary: Ireland provides public healthcare via HSE. GP Visit Card available for 70+ (free GP visits). Hospital care free in public hospitals (may wait). Private insurance (VHI, Laya) common for faster access.

costs.notes: €50-65/visit, ~1/month. Free with GP Visit Card at 70+.
Private for AMD monitoring. Public wait 6-12 months.
Quarterly blood tests. Some free via GP, others private.
€50-65/visit. Free at 70+ with GP Visit Card.

blackHairCare.label: Hair Care
blackHairCare.notes: Afro hair salons in Dublin and Cork. Growing number of diversity-focused salons.

discounts.name: Free Travel Pass
discounts.description: Free public transport (bus, rail, DART, Luas) for all residents 66+. Covers entire country.

discounts.name: Household Benefits Package
discounts.description: Free electricity/gas allowance and free TV licence for 70+ (or 66+ on certain payments).

discounts.name: GP Visit Card
discounts.description: Free GP visits for all residents 70+. Under 70 available if income below threshold.

discounts.name: Drug Payment Scheme
discounts.description: Maximum €80/month family copay on prescription drugs. Medical card holders (means-tested) pay nothing.

discounts.name: Heritage Card Senior
discounts.description: Reduced entry at OPW heritage sites for 60+. Some sites free.
